data.details: A fund acquired newly issued shares of VGI Public Company Limited ("VGI") on December 3, 2024, resulting in such fund’s shareholding exceeding each five percent threshold of the total voting rights of the business. In addition, Mr. Yim Leak was such fund’s Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO). Accordingly, Mr. Yim was required to report such acquisition of securities of VGI (Form 246-2) to the SEC Office within December 9, 2024. However, he failed to submit the report to the SEC Office
data.remark: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official.

data.company_name: Finansia Syrus Securities Public Company Limited
data.details: Between April 1, 2024 and December 21, 2025 Finansia Syrus Securities Public Company Limited as a securities broker, failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification by putting in place the inadequate and ineffective know-your-client/Customer Due Diligence (KYC/CDD) and enhanced KYC/CDD procedures.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official.

data.details: Between 1 September 2022 and 15 September 2024 Mr. Pichet Sithi-amnuai, as Managing Director of Bualuang Securities Public Company Limited (“BLS”) and the person responsible for its operations, is liable for the case in which BLS had inadequate supervisory controls over investments held as assets of its executives, resulting in insufficient safeguards against conflicts of interest. Specifically, Bualuang Securities issued internal approvals exempting six executives from the firm’s rules governing trading by executives and employees.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 100,000.00 Baht according to 97/2569

data.details: Mr. Praphol Milintachinda acquired securities of BEYOND SECURITIES PUBLIC COMPANY LIMITED (“BYD”) and, as his shareholding reached each threshold of five percent of the total voting rights of the company, he was required to report such acquisition by filing a Report of Acquisition or Disposal of Securities (Form 246-2) with the Securities and Exchange Commission within September 13, 2023. However, Mr. Praphol submitted the Form 246-2 on June 13, 2025, which constitutes a late filing beyond the period prescribed by the Capital Market Supervisory Board.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 93,900.00 Baht according to 96/2569

data.details: Mr. Anuwat Jarukornsakul, a former Chief Executive Officer of WHA Real Estate Management company limited, who is responsible for the operation of WHA Premium Growth Freehold And Leasehold Real Estate Investment Trust has changed his securities holding. In this regard, Mr. Anuwat has the duty to prepare and disclose a report regarding his holding securities and the changes to such holdings (Form 59 REIT/IFT) within December 14, 2021 and January 4, 2024. However, Mr. Anuwat prepared and disclosed the report (Form 59 REIT/IFT) to the SEC office on December 20, 2021 and January 11, 2024.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 6,150.00 Baht according to 95/2569

data.details: Miss Hathaichanok  Suwanjang, Chief Technology Officer of Humanica Public Company Limited  [“HUMAN”], has changed their securities holding, that was purchased HUMAN shares on June 7, 2022. In this regard, Miss Hathaichanok has the duty to prepare and disclose a report regarding her holding securities and the chances to such holding [From 59] within June 10, 2022. However, Miss Hathaichanok prepare and disclose a report [From 59] to the SEC office on June 13, 2022 which means Miss Hathaichanok failed to prepare and disclose the report within the period specified in the notification of the SEC.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 38,400.00 Baht according to 94/2569

data.details: Miss Sutida  Mongkolsuthree, Director of the Next Capital Public Company Limited  [“NCAP”], has the duty to prepare and disclose a report regarding her holding securities and the changes to such holding [From 59] purchased NCAP securities, receive the transfer of warrant and sold NCAP securities from Sutida’s spouse on June 23, 2022, on June 27, 2022 and on October 5, 2022. However, Miss Sutida prepare and disclose a report [From 59] to the SEC office on July 15, 2022 and on October 12, 2022 which means Miss Sutida failed to prepare and disclose the report within the period specified in the notification of the SEC.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 47,700.00 Baht according to 93/2569

data.details: Miss Kanthima  Jangwansook, Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director of SIAMRAJATHANEE Public Company Limited  [“SO”], has changed their securities holding, that was purchased SO shares on May 17, 2022. In this regard, Miss Kanthima has the duty to prepare and disclose a report regarding her holding securities and the chances to such holding [From 59] within November 22, 2022. However, Miss Kanthima prepare and disclose a report [From 59] to the SEC office on December 16, 2022 which means Miss Kanthima failed to prepare and disclose the report within the period specified in the notification of the SEC.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 6,200.00 Baht according to 92/2569

data.details: Mr. Suchet Ritteephamorn, Director of AssetWise Public Company Limited (“ASW”) was required to prepare and submit a report on the change in securities holdings (Form 59) in accordance with the SEC’s regulations, as he had conducted a transaction involving the sale of ASW warrants on 30 May 2022. However, Mr. Suchet failed to prepare and disclose the report (Form 59) to the SEC office within the period specified in the notification of SEC, which was 2 June 2022, as he submitted the Form 59 report to the SEC on 17 June 2022, which was later than the deadlines prescribed in the relevant SEC notification.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 5,750.00 Baht according to 91/2569

data.details: Mr. Sukum Tokaranyaset, Director of Khon Kaen Sugar Industry Public Company Limited (“KSL”) was required to prepare and submit a report on the change in securities holdings (Form 59) in accordance with the SEC’s regulations, as he had conducted a transaction involving the sale of KSL securities on 15 June 2022. However, Mr. Sukhum failed to prepare and disclose the report (Form 59) to the SEC office within the period specified in the notification of SEC, which was 20 June 2022, as he submitted the Form 59 report to the SEC on 2 August 2022, which was later than the deadlines prescribed in the relevant SEC notification.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 14,300.00 Baht according to 90/2569

data.details: Mr Vorathep Wongsasuthikul, Chairman of Thai Rubber Latex Group Public Company Limited (“TRUBB”), had changes in his securities holdings in TRUBB on 30 June 2025, through a transaction executed by himself to purchase convertible TRUBB shares, as well as a transaction executed by his spouse or cohabitant. Accordingly, Mr. Vorathep had the duty to prepare and disclose a report on the changes in his securities holdings (Form 59) to the SEC within the period specified in the notification of the SEC, which was 3 July 2025. However, Mr. Vorathep failed to prepare and disclose the Form 59 report to the SEC office within the prescribed period, as he submitted the report on 7 July 2025, which was later than the deadlines specified in the relevant SEC notification.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 38,700.00 Baht according to 89/2569

data.details: Mr Pipope Chokvathana, Director of Saha Pathana Inter-Holding Public Company Limited (“SPI”) was required to prepare and submit a report on the change in securities holdings (Form 59) in accordance with the SEC’s regulations, as he had conducted a transaction involving the transfer of SPI shares from his father on 31 May 2022. However, Mr. Phiphop failed to prepare and disclose the report (Form 59) to the SEC office within the period specified in the notification of SEC (by 6 June 2022), as he submitted the Form 59 report to the SEC on 13 June 2022, which was later than the deadlines prescribed in the relevant SEC notification.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 39,600.00 Baht according to 88/2569

data.details: Mr. Santipap Charasphaew, a head of compliance department of a digital asset broker called Bitazza Company Limited (''Bitazza'') at the time of the incident, has been responsible for the operation of Bitazza. His failure to perform the required duties of his position resulted in the offence committed by Bitazza concerning the case that during May 25, 2025 – September 18, 2025, Bitazza, a digital asset broker, failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification. In this regard, Bitazza's compliance system for monitoring the operations of the digital asset business operator was deficient. In particular, Bitazza failed to prepare an audit plan that appropriately reflected the relevant risks. The audit procedures were also inadequate. In addition, the reporting of audit results was ineffective.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 693,000.00 Baht according to 12/2569

data.details: Mr. Tanawat Sutuntivorakoon, a Chief Executive Officer of a digital asset broker called Bitazza Company Limited (''Bitazza'') at the time of the incident, was responsible for the operation of Bitazza. His failure to perform the required duties of his position resulted in the offence committed by Bitazza concerning the case that during May 25, 2025 – September 3, 2025 Bitazza failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification. In this regard, Bitazza failed to provide appropriate internal systems and processes for preparing, approving, and supervising advertisements. In particular, Bitazza's advertisement review process was inadequate. As a result, advertisements published during this period contained contents that could encourage or imply guaranteed returns from investments in digital assets, based on inappropriate assumptions or supporting information. In addition, Bitazza did not provide any channels for investors to obtain further information about digital assets. The advertisements also failed to include required warning statements or provide an audible presentation of such warnings. Furthermore, cryptocurrency advertisements were not restricted to Bitazza's official communication channels.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 633,000.00 Baht according to 11/2569

data.details: During May 25, 2025 – September 3, 2025, Bitazza Company Limited (''Bitazza''), a digital asset broker, failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification. In this regard, Bitazza failed to provide appropriate internal systems and processes for preparing, approving, and supervising advertisements. In particular, Bitazza's advertisement review process was inadequate. As a result, advertisements published during this period contained contents that could encourage or imply guaranteed returns from investments in digital assets, based on inappropriate assumptions or supporting information. In addition, Bitazza did not provide any channels for investors to obtain further information about digital assets. The advertisements also failed to include required warning statements or provide an audible presentation of such warnings. Furthermore, cryptocurrency advertisements were not restricted to Bitazza's official communication channels.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 633,000.00 Baht according to 10/2569

data.details: During May 25, 2025 – September 18, 2025, Bitazza Company Limited (''Bitazza''), a digital asset broker, failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification. In this regard, Bitazza's compliance system for monitoring the operations of the digital asset business operator was deficient. In particular, Bitazza failed to prepare an audit plan that appropriately reflected the relevant risks. The audit procedures were also inadequate. In addition, the reporting of audit results was ineffective.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 693,000.00 Baht according to 10/2569

data.details: During 14 May 2021 – 17 June 2022, IBS Intensive Co., Ltd. and Mr. Kridtapon Petkeawna jointly provided services or held themself out to the public that they would invest funds in gold futures. They also engaged in solicitation and publicity activities through a Facebook page named Kridtapon Petkeawna. Accordingly, such conduct constitutes operating as a derivatives fund manager without obtaining the required licenses.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official.

data.details: During 14 May 2021 – 17 June 2022, IBS Intensive Co., Ltd. and Mr. Kridtapon Petkeawna jointly provided services or held themself out to the public that they would invest funds in gold futures. They also engaged in solicitation and publicity activities through a Facebook page named Kridtapon Petkeawna. Accordingly, such conduct constitutes operating as a derivatives fund manager without obtaining the required licenses.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official.
data.relevant_section: 16 in conjunction with Section 83 of the Penal Code / SEC Act

data.details: During 17 November 2021 – 3 May 2022, IBS Intensive Co., Ltd. provided services or held itself out to the public that it would invest funds in digital assets. The company also engaged in solicitation and publicity activities through a Facebook page named Kridtapon Petkeawna. Such conduct constitutes operating as a digital asset fund manager without obtaining the required licenses.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official.

data.details: Mr. Kridtapon Petkeawna, a director of IBS Intensive Co., Ltd. (''IBS Intensive''), has been responsible for the operation of IBS Intensive during the relevant period. His order or action resulted in IBS Intensive's offence regarding the digital asset fund manager without a license owing to, during 17 November 2021 – 3 May 2022, providing services or holding itself out to the public that it would invest funds in digital assets. Accordingly, Mr. Kridtapon shall also be liable for the penalties as provided for IBS Intensive's offence.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official.
data.relevant_section: 94 in conjunction with Section 26 of the Penal Code / DAB Act

data.details: failure to perform duties or provide services with honesty and good faith, by misappropriating investor assets
data.action_taken: Revocation

data.details: During 24 April 2018 – 5 October 2018, Mr. Saravut Udomtham colluded with 12 persons to manipulate the securities price of East Coast Furnitech Public Company Limited ("ECF")'s shares by placing trading orders or trading ECF's shares in such a way that deceive investors by controlling or artificially affecting the price or volume of the securities, and placing trading orders or trading securities on a continued basis with an intent to cause the price or the volume of such securities trading to be inconsistent with the normal market condition. In this regard, Suppharoek assisted or facilitated Thanaris and Audcharapron with such actions.
data.action_taken: 05/05/2026 agreed to comply with civil penalties determined by the Civil Penalty Committee as follows :- a compensation of the benefit received from committing the offense in an amount of  1,345,940.00 Baht- a civil monetary penalty of 2,018,910.00 Baht- a suspension of securities and derivatives trading between 20/12/2027 and 04/09/2028- a bar from serving as a director or executive in a securities issuing company between 05/08/2029 and 04/01/2031- a bar from serving as a director or executive in a securities company between 05/08/2029 and 04/01/2031- a reimbursement of investigative expenses in an amount of 13,003.00 Baht
data.relevant_section: 244/3 in conjunction with 244/5 and 244/6 in conjunction with Section 83 of the Penal Code / SEC Act (the fifth amendment)

data.action_taken: 05/05/2026 agreed to comply with civil penalties determined by the Civil Penalty Committee as follows :- a civil monetary penalty of 500,000.00 Baht- a suspension of securities and derivatives trading between 05/05/2026 and 19/01/2027- a bar from serving as a director or executive in a securities issuing company between 05/05/2026 and 04/10/2027- a bar from serving as a director or executive in a securities company between 05/05/2026 and 04/10/2027- a reimbursement of investigative expenses in an amount of 10,782.00 Baht
data.relevant_section: 244/3 in conjunction with 244/5 and 244/6 in conjunction with Section 83 of the Penal Code / SEC Act (the fifth amendment)

data.details: During 24 August 2017 – 21 December 2017, Mr. Phuthitorn Tamthum colluded with 15 persons to manipulate the securities price of East Coast Furnitech Public Company Limited ("ECF")'s shares by placing trading orders or trading ECF's shares in such a way that deceive investors by controlling or artificially affecting the price or volume of the securities, and placing trading orders or trading securities on a continued basis with an intent to cause the price or the volume of such securities trading to be inconsistent with the normal market condition.
data.action_taken: 05/05/2026 agreed to comply with civil penalties determined by the Civil Penalty Committee as follows :- a civil monetary penalty of 500,000.00 Baht- a suspension of securities and derivatives trading between 05/05/2026 and 19/01/2027- a bar from serving as a director or executive in a securities issuing company between 05/05/2026 and 04/10/2027- a bar from serving as a director or executive in a securities company between 05/05/2026 and 04/10/2027- a reimbursement of investigative expenses in an amount of 10,782.00 Baht
data.relevant_section: 244/3 in conjunction with 244/5 in conjunction with Section 83 of the Penal Code / SEC Act (the fifth amendment)

data.details: Ms. Chintana Kittiviboolmas, Managing Director, who is responsible for the operation of United Overseas Bank (Thai) Public Company Limited ("UOB") shall be liable for UOB’s failure to prepare and submit the report on the sale results of structured notes offered to Institutional or High Net Worth investors (II&HNW) through the transmission system of the SEC Office as prescribed by the Notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission Board, due to her failure to order or act in accordance with duties required to be performed.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 10,360.00 Baht according to 87/2569

data.company_name: United Overseas Bank (Thai) Public Company Limited
data.details: United Overseas Bank (Thai) Public Company Limited ("UOB"), failed to prepare and submit the report on the sale results of structured notes offered to Institutional or High Net Worth investors (II&HNW) through the transmission system of the SEC Office the period specified in the notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission Board on November 29, 2024. However, the report on the sale results of structured notes offered to institutional or High Net Worth Investors (II&HNW) was later submitted through the transmission system of the SEC Office on December 2, 2024.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 10,360.00 Baht according to 86/2569

data.details: Energy Mahanakhon Company limited ("EMN") colluded with Mr. Phornlert Techarattanopas and Miss Yaowalug Pukpikul offered to sale EMN stock as a newly issued securities in the category of shares (Public Offering) without approval from the SEC office which is in breach of the Securities and Exchange Act B.E. 2535.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 57,089,994.00 Baht according to 85/2569
data.relevant_section: 34 in conjunction with Section 83 of the Penal Code / SEC Act

data.details: During 26 September 2018 to 7 February 2019, Miss Pornpin Chivikrai failed to report her acquisition and disposition of Thai Capital Corporation Public Company Limited (“TCC”) shares held in her and other 4 people’s securities account to the SEC Office within the period specified in the notification of the Capital Market Supervisory Board when her aggregated holding of TCC shares reached or passed five percent of the total number of voting rights of TCC. Miss Pornpin later filed the accurate reports (Form 246-2) to the SEC Office on 8 July 2025.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 295,300.00 Baht according to 84/2569

data.details: Mr. Chone Sophonpanich, while held the position of Director and Executive Officer of Bangkok Life Assurance Public Company Limited (“BLA”). His wife, Ms. Prapavadee Sophonpanich, had changed her securities holding of BLA on 9 July 2021 and 27 Febuary 2024. In this regard, Mr. Chone had duty to prepare and disclose the report regarding Ms. Prapavadee's holding securities and the changes to such holdings (Form 59) within 14 September 2021 and 30 August 2024 respectively. However, Mr. Chone prepared and disclosed the report (Form 59) to the SEC office on 4 April 2022 and 30 May 2025 respectively which is later than the specified period.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 270,700.00 Baht according to 83/2569

data.details: Mr. Chaiyut Srivikorn, a director of CPANEL Public Company Limited ("CPANEL"), has changed his own securities holding between 15 September 2021 to 7 April 2022. In this regard, Mr. Chaiyut has duty to prepare and disclose a report regarding his holding securities and the changes to such holdings (Form 59) between 30 September 2021 and 12 April 2022, depending on circumstances. However, Mr. Chaiyut prepared and disclosed the report (Form 59) to the SEC office on 24 June 2022, later than the specified period.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 149,200.00 Baht according to 82/2569

data.details: Mr. Chih-Che Yen, Chairman of Hwa Fong Rubber (Thailand) Public Company Limited (HFT), has changed his securities holding on September, 2021. In this regard, Mr. Chih-Che has the duty to prepare and disclose a report regarding his holding securities and the changes to such holdings (Form 59) to the SEC office within September 8, 2021. However, Mr. Chih-Che prepared and disclosed the report (Form 59) to the SEC office on September 20, 2021.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 41,100.00 Baht according to 81/2569

data.details: Mr. Wang Wanapaison, Chief Executive Officer and Director of Applied DB Public Company Limited (ADB), has changed his securities holding on August 24, 2021, and September 19, 2023. In this regard, Mr. Wang has the duty to prepare and disclose a report regarding his holding securities and the changes to such holdings (Form 59) to the SEC office within August 27, 2021, and September 22, 2023. However, Mr. Wang prepared and disclosed the report (Form 59) to the SEC office on September 3, 2021 and September 29, 2023, which means Mr. Wang failed to prepare and disclose the report within the period specified in the notification of the Capital Market.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 44,950.00 Baht according to 80/2569

data.details: Mr. Wijit Jiemwijitkul, holding the position of Chief Executive Officer responsible for the operations of Thung Kha Harbor Public Company Limited (“THL”), shall be liable for THL’s failure to prepare and submit the annual financial statements for the year 2021 within the period specified by the Capital Market Supervisory Board. This liability arises from his failure to issue orders or perform the duties required to ensure compliance with the specified requirements, resulting in THL’s delayed submission of the annual financial statements
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 82,800.00 Baht according to 78/2569

data.details: Mr. Wijit Jiemwijitkul, holding the position of Chief Executive Officer responsible for the operations of Thung Kha Harbor Public Company Limited (“THL”), shall be liable for THL’s failure to prepare and submit the Management’s Discussion and Analysis (MD&A) for the year 2021 within the period specified by the Capital Market Supervisory Board. This liability arises from his failure to issue orders or perform the duties required to ensure compliance with the specified requirements, resulting in THL’s delayed submission of the Management’s Discussion and Analysis (MD&A) for the year 2021
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 82,800.00 Baht according to 78/2569

data.details: Mr. Wijit Jiemwijitkul, holding the position of Chief Executive Officer responsible for the operations of Thung Kha Harbor Public Company Limited (“THL”), shall be liable for THL’s failure to prepare and submit the Annual Registration Statement / Annual Report (“Form 56-1 One Report”) for the year 2021 within the period specified by the Capital Market Supervisory Board. This liability arises from his failure to issue orders or perform the duties required to ensure compliance with the specified requirements, resulting in THL’s delayed submission of the Form 56-1 One Report for the year 2021
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 95,400.00 Baht according to 78/2569

data.company_name: Thung Kha Harbor Public Company Limited
data.details: Thung Kha Harbor Public Company Limited ("THL") prepared and submitted its annual financial statements for 2021 later than the deadline prescribed by the Capital Market Supervisory Board, which is 31 March 2022. However, THL submitted such financial statements to the SEC Office on 2 June 2022, thereby submitting its annual financial statements for 2021 after the prescribed deadline
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 82,800.00 Baht according to 77/2569

data.company_name: Thung Kha Harbor Public Company Limited

However, THL submitted such report to the SEC Office on 2 June 2022, thereby submitting its Management’s Discussion and Analysis (MD&A) for the year 2021 after the prescribed deadline
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 82,800.00 Baht according to 77/2569

data.company_name: Thung Kha Harbor Public Company Limited

data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 95,400.00 Baht according to 77/2569

data.details: Mr. Visit Malaisirirat, a former Chief Executive Officer, who is responsible for the operation of Magnolia Quality Development Corporation Limited ("MQDC"), shall be liable for MQDC’s failure to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification of the Capital Market Supervisory Board which is MQDC used the proceeds obtained from the debenture offering,. which is inconsistent with the objective to use the proceeds identified in the registration statement and the draft prospectus, due to his failure to order or act in accordance with duties required to be performed.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 421,800.00 Baht according to 63/2569

data.details: Magnolia Quality Development Corporation Limited ("MQDC") used the proceeds obtained from the debenture offering, which is inconsistent with the objective to use the proceeds identified in the registration statement and the draft prospectus that is not in accordance with rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification of the Capital Market Supervisory Board.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 421,800.00 Baht according to 62/2569

data.details: During February 21, 2025 to October 26, 2025, Coindee Company Limited, a digital asset advisory at the time of the incident, failed to maintain the net capital in accordance with the notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 3,240,000.00 Baht according to 9/2569

data.details: Coindee Company Limited ("Coindee"), a digital asset advisory at the time of the incident, failed to prepare and submit the NC-3 report for the first half of the year 2025 to the SEC office within the period specified in the notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission. In this regard, Coindee did not take any action within July 7, 2025 until its license was revoked.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 372,000.00 Baht according to 9/2569

data.details: During March 1, 2025 to October 26, 2025, Coindee Company Limited, a digital asset advisory at the time of the incident, did not have an appropriate and sufficient management structure, operating systems, and personnel for conducting business in accordance with the rules, procedures, and conditions prescribed by the Securities and Exchange Commission.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 564,000.00 Baht according to 9/2569

data.details: During April 28, 2025 to October 26, 2025, Coindee Company Limited, a digital asset advisory at the time of the incident, appointed an authorized director without obtaining approval from the SEC office.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 514,000.00 Baht according to 9/2569

data.details: Coindee Company Limited ("Coindee"), a digital asset advisory at the time of the incident, had duty to submit an annual financial statement of the year 2024 to the SEC office within 4 months since the end of the accounting period, as well as publishing such financial statement on the company’s website within such period. In this regard, Coindee submitted such financial statement on September 19, 2025, later than the period specified in the notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission. Moreover, Coindee failed to publish such financial statement on the company’s website until its license was revoked.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 269,000.00 Baht according to 9/2569

data.details: Miss Neeranuch Kosolthanurawet, a former Chief Operation Officer of a digital asset fund manager called Merkle Capital Co., Ltd. ("Merkle"), was responsible for the operation of Merkle relating to customers' assets. Her failure to perform the required duty of her position resulted in the offence committed by Merkle concerning the case that, from March 1, 2022 to April 21, 2025, Merkle failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission. In this regard, Merkle’s 11 bank accounts used to deposit customer cash were under the company's name without the word “for customer”. Accordingly, it was not clearly indicated that the accounts are held by the company for the benefit of customers.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 389,000.00 Baht according to 8/2569
data.relevant_section: 94 in conjunction with 30 and 31 / DAB Act

data.details: From March 1, 2022 to April 21, 2025, Merkle Capital Co., Ltd. ("Merkle") as a digital asset fund manager, failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission. In this regard, Merkle’s 11 bank accounts used to deposit customer cash were under the company's name without the word “for customer”. Accordingly, it was not clearly indicated that the accounts are held by the company for the benefit of customers.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 389,000.00 Baht according to 7/2569

data.details: Mr. Att Tongyai Asavanund, a Chief Executive Officer of a digital asset exchange called ERX Company Limited ("ERX"), has been responsible for the operation of ERX. His failure to perform the required duty of his position resulted in the offence committed by ERX concerning the case that ,on April 25, 2025, May 31, 2025 and June 28, 2025, ERX failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission. In this regard, ERX failed to check the allocated foreign exchange limit prior to executing transactions. Accordingly, ERX conducted foreign currency transactions exceeding the approved limit set by the SEC office.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 108,000.00 Baht according to 6/2569

data.details: On April 25, 2025, May 31, 2025 and June 28, 2025, ERX Company Limited ("ERX") as a digital asset exchange, failed to comply with the rules, conditions and procedures as specified in the notification of the Securities and Exchange Commission. In this regard, ERX failed to check the allocated foreign exchange limit prior to executing transactions. Accordingly, ERX conducted foreign currency transactions exceeding the approved limit set by the SEC office.
data.action_taken: imposing a fine of 108,000.00 Baht according to 5/2569

data.details: During 20 September 2021 – 4 March 2025, Exmix Company Limited (''Exmix'') as the operator of the websites https://www.exmix.co and https://wallet.exmix.io/, provided services or held itself out to the public as available to provide services with respect to the trading and exchange of digital assets for Exmix's own account in the normal course of business outside the digital asset exchange. In addition, Exmix solicited and promoted the use of its services through the aforementioned websites via social media channels in the Thai language. Accordingly, such conduct constitutes operating as a digital asset dealer without obtaining the required license.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official.

data.details: Mr. Chatpon Sakrucharat, a director of Exmix Company Limited (''Exmix''), has been responsible for the operation of Exmix during the relevant period. His order or action resulted in Exmix's offence regarding the digital asset dealer without a license owing to, during 20 September 2021 – 4 March 2025, providing services or holding itself out to the public as available to provide services with respect to the trading and exchange of digital assets for Exmix’s own account in the normal course of business outside the digital asset exchange. In addition, Exmix solicited and promoted the use of its services through the aforementioned websites via social media channels in the Thai language. Accordingly, Mr. Chatpon shall also be liable for the penalties as provided for Exmix's offence.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official.
data.relevant_section: 94 in conjunction with 26 and 83 of the Penal Code / DAB Act

data.details: Mr. Nantawat Samruanhant, the auditor of Stark Corporation Public Company Limited ("STARK"), failed to  comply with auditing standards in relation to audit work performed for the financial statements for the year 2021 of STARK and two subsidiaries that are significant to the consolidated financial statements, namely Phelps Dodge International (Thailand) Limited (PDITL) and Adisorn Songkla Company Limited (ADS).The above failure to comply with auditing standards constituted a facilitating factor for fraud at STARK and its subsidiaries, as well as the falsification of STARK’s financial statements.

Bitazza Company Limited ("Bitazza") provided a service known as "Crypto Dust" to the public by exchanging digital assets with Bitazza itself in the normal course of business and charging fees. Also, there was no evidence indicating that purchase or sale orders were routed through any digital asset exchange. Instead, such activities were conducted outside any digital asset exchange. This Bitazza’s service shall be deemed to be the operation of a digital asset dealer without a license. In addition, Bitazza and Freedomverse Company Limited ("FreedomVerse"), jointly provided the product known as "Freedom Card", which involved purchasing digital assets in exchange for cash and crediting such cash to Freedom Card, with fees charged. Furthermore, they solicited the public to use their services through Bitazza’s Facebook account, demonstrating an intention to provide services to customers in public. In this regard, there was no evidence indicating that purchase or sale orders were routed through any digital asset exchange. Instead, such service was conducted outside any digital asset exchange. The aforementioned action of Bitazza and FreedomVerse shall be deemed to be the operation of a digital asset dealer without a license.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official

data.details: Mr. Kavin Phongpandecha, a former Chief Executive Officer of Bitazza Company Limited ("Bitazza") was responsible for the operation of Bitazza during the relevant period. His order or action resulted in Bitazza’s offence regarding the digital asset dealer without a license owing to providing a service known as "Crypto Dust" and joint providing the product known as "Freedom Card" with Freedomverse Company Limited. Accordingly, Mr. Kavin shall also be liable for the penalties as provided for Bitazza’s offence.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official
data.relevant_section: 94 in conjunction with 26 and 83 of the Penal Code / DAB Act

data.details: Mr. Tanawat Sutuntivorakoon, a Chief Executive Officer of Bitazza Company Limited ("Bitazza"), has been responsible for the operation of Bitazza during the relevant period. His order or action resulted in Bitazza’s offence regarding the digital asset dealer without a license owing to joint providing the product known as "Freedom Card" with Freedomverse Company Limited. Accordingly, Mr. Tanawat shall also be liable for the penalties as provided for Bitazza’s offence.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official
data.relevant_section: 94 in conjunction with 26 and 83 of the Penal Code / DAB Act

data.details: Freedomverse Company Limited ("FreedomVerse") and Bitazza Company Limited ("Bitazza") jointly provided the product known as "Freedom Card", which involved purchasing digital assets in exchange for cash and crediting such cash to Freedom Card, with fees charged. Furthermore, they solicited the public to use their services through Bitazza’s Facebook account, demonstrating an intention to provide services to customers in public. In this regard, there was no evidence indicating that purchase or sale orders were routed through any digital asset exchange. Instead, such service was conducted outside any digital asset exchange. The aforementioned action of Bitazza and FreedomVerse shall be deemed to be the operation of a digital asset dealer without a license.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official

data.details: Mr. Kavin Phongpandecha and Mr. Wee Kiat Kevin Heng as directors of Freedomverse Company Limited ("FreedomVerse") and signatories to an agreement between Bitazza Company Limited ("Bitazza") and FreedomVerse were deemed the person responsible for the operation of FreedomVerse at the time of the incident. Their orders or actions resulted in FreedomVerse’s offence regarding the digital asset dealer without a license owing to joint providing the product known as "Freedom Card" with Bitazza Company Limited. Accordingly, Mr. Kavin and Mr. Wee Kiat Kevin Heng shall also be liable for the penalties as provided for FreedomVerse’s offence.
data.action_taken: This case is in the process of inquiry by the inquiry official
data.relevant_section: 94 in conjunction with 26 and 83 of the Penal Code / DAB Act
